 {"id":"bd-hj0s","title":"Add 'convoy' issue type with reactive completion","description":"Add convoy as a new issue type with reactive completion semantics.

Behavior:
- Convoy has list of tracked issues (via 'tracks' relation)
- When all tracked issues close (including wontfix), convoy auto-closes
- Supports cross-prefix tracking (convoy in hq-* tracks gt-*, bd-*)

Implementation:
- New type: convoy
- Reactive completion trigger on tracked issue closure
- Query support: 'bd list --type=convoy'

Related: hq-7h8jx (Convoy System epic in town beads)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"beads/crew/dave","created_at":"2025-12-29T18:47:02.011011-08:00","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2025-12-30T00:05:13.515078-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T00:05:13.515078-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented convoy type with reactive completion","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hj0s","depends_on_id":"bd-3roq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-29T18:47:10.59211-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-hobo","title":"Distinct prefixes for protos, molecules, wisps","description":"Template/workflow entities should have visually distinct prefixes from regular issues.

**Problem:**
- Protos (bd-7bs4) look like regular issues - invites squashing
- Molecules (poured instances) also use bd- prefix
- Wisps are in separate DB but still use bd- when referenced

**Proposed Prefixes:**
- `proto-` for templates (e.g., proto-release, proto-review)
- `mol-` for active molecules (poured from protos)
- `wisp-` for ephemeral wisps (vapor phase)

**Benefits:**
- Instant visual recognition of entity type
- Prevents accidental modification of templates
- Clear lifecycle: proto → mol → wisp → digest

**Implementation options:**
1. Separate databases with different prefixes
2. Issue type determines prefix generation
3. Naming convention enforced by bd pour/wisp commands","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-24T16:45:24.940809-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T02:04:52.459233-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-25T02:04:52.459233-08:00","labels":["workflow"]}
@@ -809,7 +809,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-ykqu","title":"Add gate timeout tracking and notification","description":"Implement timeout and notification logic for gates.

## Timeout Behavior
1. Gate created with timeout (e.g., 30m)
2. Deacon tracks elapsed time during patrol
3. If timeout reached:
   - Notify all waiters: "Gate timed out"
   - Close gate with timeout reason
   - Waiter can retry, escalate, or fail gracefully

## Notification
- Use gt mail send to notify waiters
- Include gate ID, await type, and reason in message
- Support multiple waiters notification

## Escalation Path
- Witness sees stuck worker, nudges them
- Worker can escalate to human if needed","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T11:44:40.1825-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T12:19:44.362527-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T12:19:44.362527-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ykqu","depends_on_id":"bd-udsi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T11:44:53.072862-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-ykqu","depends_on_id":"bd-is6m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-23T11:44:56.595085-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-ymqn","title":"Code review: bd mol bond --ref and bd activity (bd-xo1o work)","description":"Review dave's recent commits for bd-xo1o (Dynamic Molecule Bonding):

## Commits to Review
- ee04b1ea: feat: add dynamic molecule bonding with --ref flag (bd-xo1o.1)
- be520d90: feat: add bd activity command for real-time state feed (bd-xo1o.3)

## Review Focus
1. Code quality and correctness
2. Error handling
3. Edge cases
4. Test coverage
5. Documentation

## Deliverables
- File beads for any issues found
- Note any concerns or suggestions
- Verify the implementation matches the bd-xo1o epic requirements","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T03:47:55.217363-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T04:11:00.226326-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T04:11:00.226326-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-yqhh","title":"bd list --parent: filter by parent issue","description":"Add --parent flag to bd list to filter issues by parent.

Example:
```bash
bd list --parent=gt-h5n --status=open
```

Would show all open children of gt-h5n.

Useful for:
- Checking epic progress
- Finding swarmable work within an epic
- Molecule step listing","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-23T01:51:26.830952-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T02:10:12.909803-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T02:10:12.909803-08:00"}
-{"id":"bd-yuxq","title":"Code smell: 262 uses of interface{} reduce type safety","description":"Heavy use of interface{} across 92 files (262 occurrences) in:
- RPC update arguments: updatesFromArgs() returns map[string]interface{}
- JSON unmarshaling and formatting
- Storage query helpers

**Example in internal/rpc/server_issues_epics.go:45:**
```go
func updatesFromArgs(a UpdateArgs) map[string]interface{} {
    // Returns map with interface{} values - types must be asserted at use sites
}
```

**Problem:**
- Loss of type safety
- Runtime type assertions required
- Harder to catch errors at compile time

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Define typed update structs for specific field groups
- [ ] Use generics where possible (Go 1.18+)
- [ ] Reduce interface{} count by at least 50%
- [ ] Document expected types where interface{} remains necessary
- [ ] Tests pass","status":"hooked","priority":3,"issue_type":"chore","assignee":"beads/polecats/opal","created_at":"2025-12-28T19:00:01.896147-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/dave","updated_at":"2025-12-30T00:24:24.11404-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-yuxq","depends_on_id":"bd-ox1o","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-28T19:00:23.812412-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"bd-yuxq","title":"Code smell: 262 uses of interface{} reduce type safety","description":"Heavy use of interface{} across 92 files (262 occurrences) in:
- RPC update arguments: updatesFromArgs() returns map[string]interface{}
- JSON unmarshaling and formatting
- Storage query helpers

**Example in internal/rpc/server_issues_epics.go:45:**
```go
func updatesFromArgs(a UpdateArgs) map[string]interface{} {
    // Returns map with interface{} values - types must be asserted at use sites
}
```

**Problem:**
- Loss of type safety
- Runtime type assertions required
- Harder to catch errors at compile time

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Define typed update structs for specific field groups
- [ ] Use generics where possible (Go 1.18+)
- [ ] Reduce interface{} count by at least 50%
- [ ] Document expected types where interface{} remains necessary
- [ ] Tests pass","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"chore","created_at":"2025-12-28T19:00:01.896147-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/dave","updated_at":"2025-12-30T00:42:12.539958-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-yuxq","depends_on_id":"bd-ox1o","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-28T19:00:23.812412-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
